behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction lists all specific strings tested: branded type names, brand literals, Layer.effect, Effect.gen patterns, AccountRepo.of, namespace patterns, header texts like '# opencode Effect guide', '## Schemas', etc.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes required patterns and symptoms but doesn't provide line-level fixes; agent still must implement the code.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert very specific literal strings like 'AccountRepo.of({', 'Effect.gen(function* ()', 'Layer.effect(', and AGENTS.md headers; alternative valid refactors using e.g. Layer.scoped or different formatting would fail string-based assertions.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute bun test and typecheck subprocesses (test_repo_passes_account_tests, test_repo_typecheck) in addition to string checks.

test_not_tautological.reason: The repo.test.ts and service.test.ts are executed via bun test and require actual implementation; stubs wouldn't pass the executed tests.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover schema branded types, service/repo Layer.effect patterns, namespace, and test execution as specified in instruction.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with clear section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass) and descriptive function names.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is a code refactor, not producing structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths and commands appear consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names modules/files that are inherently the task domain; no specific line numbers leaked.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Not an agentmd-edits task; AGENTS.md additions are a small documented section.
